title: tragacantha cracca
intro: Tragacantha cracca, also known as French or Russian knapweed, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Consuming Tragacantha cracca is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared by making teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
